---
title: "OECD and UNESCO Data Processing"
format: html
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Setup
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Specify data directory
data_dir <- Sys.getenv("data_dir")

# Add source setup file
source("setup.R")
```

# Load and Process Data
```{r load-data}
# Open GERD data
gerd_data <- read_csv(file.path(data_dir, "r2", "btpsych_gerd_data.csv"))

# Open researchers data (skip first 4 rows)
researchers_data <- read_csv(
  file.path(data_dir, "r2", "btpsych_researchers_data.csv"),
  skip = 4
)
```

# Calculate Averages for Year 2000 and Beyond

```{r calculate-averages}
# Process GERD data - calculate average for year 2000+
gerd_averages <- gerd_data |>
  filter(TIME_PERIOD >= 2000) |>
  group_by(REF_AREA) |>
  summarise(
    avg_gerd = mean(OBS_VALUE, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  rename(iso3 = REF_AREA)

# Process researchers data - calculate average for year 2000+
researchers_averages <- researchers_data |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = `1960`:`2023`,
    names_to = "year",
    values_to = "researchers_value"
  ) |>
  mutate(year = as.numeric(year)) |>
  filter(year >= 2000) |>
  group_by(`Country Code`) |>
  summarise(
    avg_researchers = mean(researchers_value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  rename(iso3 = `Country Code`)

# Combine GERD and researchers averages
combined_averages <- gerd_averages |>
  full_join(researchers_averages, by = "iso3")
```

# Load OpenAlex Data and Join
```{r join-data}
# Open OpenAlex processed data
openalex_data <- readRDS(
  file = file.path(
    data_dir,
    "worlddata",
    "DF.o.w.Rds"
  )
)

# Join with combined averages using iso3 codes
final_data <- openalex_data |>
  left_join(combined_averages, by = "iso3")

# View summary of joined data
final_data |>
  select(iso3, avg_gerd, avg_researchers) |>
  summary()

rm(
  combined_averages,
  gerd_averages,
  researchers_averages,
  gerd_data,
  researchers_data,
  openalex_data
)
```

# Quick analysis

```{r}
cor.test(
  x = final_data$authors.n,
  y = final_data$avg_gerd,
  method = "spearman"
)

cor.test(
  x = final_data$authors.n,
  y = final_data$avg_researchers,
  method = "spearman"
)

# Function to do this in a Bayesian framework
bayes_rank_corr_lmBF <- function(x_var, y_var, data_name = "") {
  # Create complete cases data with ranks
  d <- final_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(!!sym(x_var)) & !is.na(!!sym(y_var))) %>%
    mutate(
      x_rank = rank(!!sym(x_var)),
      y_rank = rank(!!sym(y_var))
    ) %>%
    data.frame()

  # Bayesian correlation test
  bf_result <- lmBF(x_rank ~ y_rank, data = d)

  cat("\n---", data_name, "---\n")
  print(bf_result)
  return(bf_result)
}

bf_e_authors <- bayes_rank_corr_lmBF(
  "avg_gerd",
  "authors.n",
  "GERD ~ Authors"
)

bf_e_authors <- bayes_rank_corr_lmBF(
  "avg_researchers",
  "authors.n",
  "GERD ~ Authors"
)
```
